config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena itu membuka jalan bagi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu justru menjadi batas agar mayoritas atau negara tidak semena-mena atas nama “kepentingan umum.” Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang mampu melindungi keduanya, bukan mengorbankan salah satunya secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically outrank individual rights. From my perspective as an American, that kind of blanket priority is risky because it can easily become an excuse for the government or the majority to trample free speech, due process, privacy, and minority rights in the name of the “common good.” At the same time, I do not think individual rights are absolute in every case, since things like public health, safety, and basic laws sometimes require limits. The real standard should be whether a restriction is narrow, necessary, and accountable, not whether society simply gets to win by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu jika dimaknai bahwa kepentingan masyarakat selalu lebih tinggi daripada hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya besar ketika “kepentingan umum” dipakai sebagai alasan untuk membungkam kritik, melanggar privasi, atau mengorbankan kelompok kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu praktis. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang adil bukan yang menang terus atas individu, melainkan yang bisa menjaga kepentingan bersama tanpa menginjak martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that sounds like a dangerous blank check for governments or majorities to override free speech, privacy, due process, and minority protections whenever it is politically convenient. I do accept that rights can be limited for real public needs like safety or public health, but only when the limits are narrow, necessary, and accountable. If society always wins by default, then individual rights stop being rights and become permissions.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman kritik, diskriminasi terhadap kelompok kecil, atau kebijakan yang tidak transparan. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi harus lewat alasan yang jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diuji, bukan karena mayoritas atau pemerintah merasa paling tahu. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru yang berani melindungi hak orang yang paling rentan, bukan yang mengorbankannya atas nama efisiensi atau ketertiban.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should automatically outrank individual rights. As an American, I am wary of any argument that gives the government or the majority a blank check to override speech, privacy, due process, or minority protections just because it is framed as the "common good." That said, I do not treat individual rights as absolute in every situation; public health, safety, and rule-of-law concerns can justify limits, but only when they are narrow, necessary, and accountable. If society always comes first by default, then rights are no longer rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
